evals.prompt: git fetch upstream fails with 'fatal: No such remote upstream' — how do I add the upstream remote?
evals.expected_output: Tells the user to run git remote add upstream with the exact URL https://github.com/devicelab-dev/maestro-runner, and suggests git remote -v to verify the setup.
assertions: Response includes the exact upstream URL: https://github.com/devicelab-dev/maestro-runner
Response uses git remote add upstream <url>
Response suggests git remote -v to verify the remote was added correctly

evals.prompt: I have uncommitted changes in my branch — can I still merge from upstream, or do I have to commit them first?
evals.expected_output: Tells the user to stash their changes with git stash, perform the merge, then restore with git stash pop. Does NOT require committing the changes first.
assertions: Response includes git stash to save uncommitted changes before merging
Response includes git stash pop to restore changes after the merge
Response does NOT require the user to commit their changes as the only option
Response includes git merge upstream/main --no-ff or equivalent merge command

evals.prompt: I just merged upstream/main into my branch — what tests should I run to make sure nothing is broken?
evals.expected_output: Provides the ordered post-merge test sequence: (1) Go tests with make test from repo root, (2) TypeScript unit tests with npm run test:unit from client/typescript, (3) Python unit tests with .venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_client.py tests/test_models.py from client/python.
assertions: Response includes make test (or go test ./...) for Go server tests
Response includes npm run test:unit for TypeScript client tests, run from client/typescript
Response includes .venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_client.py tests/test_models.py for Python client tests
Response runs Go tests before client tests
